kennelTipLean.note: Nobody's tipping the Dogs right now. Coming off 38-16 and the forum's in full meltdown mode — Storm at home is a write-off before kickoff.

kennelTipQuotes.quote: We're going to play the Sharks back into form — and now we get Storm next. Lmao good luck lads.

trivia.question: Which year did the Bulldogs last beat Melbourne Storm in a finals match?
trivia.explainer: 2014 prelim final at ANZ — Dogs won 18-12 to book a grand final spot. Storm have had our number in finals every other time.

recap.question: Who racked up the most run metres for the Dogs against the Sharks?
recap.explainer: Tuala led the Dogs with 216 run metres and also bagged 2 line breaks — one of the only bright spots in a 38-16 hiding.

washup.headline: 38-16. Played them back into form, just like we said we would.
washup.vibe: Sharks were on the ropes coming in and we put them back on the couch. Four tries conceded in the first 32 minutes — game over before half-time. Tuala and Preston had a go, the rest of the side looked like they'd never met. Now Storm at home next Friday. Cool.
washup.kennelSummary: The Ciraldo job future thread is at 362 replies and climbing. Salmon, Tracey and Hayward are getting hung out to dry, and there's a real split between 'wait till King and Mann are back' and 'blow the whole thing up'. Galvin's getting defended for once — the Sexton-in-Perth kick-off has reset that whole debate.

kennelHotTakes.quote: Cameron Ciraldo is the new Dean Pay — expected to perform miracles with a sub-standard roster. Same movie, different decade.
kennelHotTakes.author: speedy2460

kennelThreadsLastGame.title: CIRALDOS JOB FUTURE
kennelThreadsLastGame.summary: 362 replies and counting. Johnnny2233's defending CC with finals appearances, Crittaraisemykids is blaming Gus's short temper, and Dingo's pivoted to the assistants. Brutal.

kennelThreadsLastGame.title: The Talk of Sexton is better than Galvin is over after this
kennelThreadsLastGame.summary: Sexton's bizarre kick-off in Perth has the Galvin haters in retreat. Nate DAWG's argument that Sexton was just a stick to beat Galvin with is gaining traction post-Sharks.
